# decisions.md — Cold Tier: Architectural & Infrastructure Decisions

> **Rules**: Supervisor-only writes. Each entry: `### YYYY-MM-DD — Title`, then **Decision**, **Why**, and **Files** (cite paths — the diff-driven pass greps this file by changed file path).

## Architecture

### 2026-06-18 — LLM-as-Renderer for HTML reports (no shell post-processor)
**Decision**: Stage 4 HTML reports are generated by a dedicated `html-report` skill that reads the preceding skill's output from conversation context and emits a complete self-contained HTML file inline. No shell post-processor or `sed` substitution step.
**Why**: `code-review` and `security-review` are built-in Claude Code skills with no custom SKILL.md — they cannot be modified to emit JSON footers. The LLM already has all review content in context, making a separate render call the simplest path. A shell post-processor would add fragile parsing with no benefit.
**Files**: .claude/skills/html-report/SKILL.md, templates/report_template.html

### 2026-06-18 — thinking-report renders decision reasoning, not review findings
**Decision**: `thinking-report` is a separate skill from `html-report`. It targets Stage 0.5–2 (brainstorming, grilling, planning) and renders a Decision box + Trade-Off Matrix + Assumptions list. `html-report` targets Stage 4 (review findings). They share CSS conventions but have different templates and triggers.
**Why**: The two outputs answer different questions — "what risk did this code introduce?" vs "why was this approach chosen?" Merging them would produce an unfocused page useful for neither.
**Files**: .claude/skills/thinking-report/SKILL.md, templates/thinking_report_template.html

### 2026-06-18 — Trade-off matrix only (no flowchart) for thinking-report MVP
**Decision**: `thinking-report` renders a Trade-Off Matrix + Decision box only. CSS flowchart (boxes + arrows) is explicitly deferred.
**Why**: The matrix answers "why this option?" directly and is simpler to generate reliably. CSS connector lines between boxes add implementation complexity for marginal readability gain over reading BRAINSTORMING_LOG.md top-to-bottom. Revisit after the matrix pilot proves value.
**Files**: .claude/skills/thinking-report/SKILL.md, templates/thinking_report_template.html

### 2026-06-18 — Pack system: additive-only, core unchanged
**Decision**: Packs extend the core framework by symlinking agent and skill files into `.claude/agents/` and `.claude/skills/` alongside core resources. Core agents (backend, frontend, common-infrastructure, qa) and core skills are never modified or replaced by packs.
**Why**: Users may combine packs (e.g. mobile + api) without conflict. Core resources are the stable base; packs are optional layers. Symlink approach means pack updates flow through `update.sh` automatically.
**Files**: setup.sh, packs/

### 2026-06-18 — Pack install via setup.sh: --pack=<name> flag + interactive prompt
**Decision**: Packs are selected at `setup.sh` time via interactive numbered list (TTY) or `--pack=<name>` flags (non-interactive/CI). No packs installed by default in non-interactive mode.
**Why**: Piped installs (curl | sh) cannot block on input; defaulting to no packs keeps the install safe and predictable. Users add packs explicitly.
**Files**: setup.sh

### 2026-07-01 — setup.sh central-clone guard checks $PWD, not $0's dirname; piped installs with flags need `sh -c "$(curl ...)" -- --pack=<name>`
**Decision**: The guard in `clone_or_verify()` that blocks running setup.sh with the central clone as target now compares `$PWD` to `SUPERVISOR_PATH`, not the script file's own directory. Also documented in README: a plain `curl ... | sh --pack=X` cannot pass flags through — `sh` parses its own CLI flags before reading the piped script and rejects `--pack=X` as unrecognized. Use `sh -c "$(curl -fsSL <url>)" -- --pack=<name>` instead (same pattern as rustup/nvm/Homebrew installers); `sh -s -- --pack=<name>` also works if piping directly.
**Why**: The old guard compared `$(dirname "$0")` to `SUPERVISOR_PATH` — since `setup.sh` always lives inside `~/.supervisor` once deployed, every direct invocation like `sh ~/.supervisor/setup.sh --pack=X` (the exact form the README told users to run) false-tripped the guard, even from a legitimate target-project cwd. Root-caused via PR #41 (branch `fix/setup-central-clone-guard`).
**Files**: setup.sh, README.md

### 2026-06-18 — Pack structure: agents/ + skills/ + PACK.md per pack
**Decision**: Each pack contains `agents/<name>.md`, `skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`, and `PACK.md`. No other file types. Pack agents use namespaced names (e.g. `mobile-developer`, not `developer`) to avoid collisions with core agents.
**Why**: Consistent structure enables `install_pack()` to iterate generically. Namespacing prevents silent overwrites.
**Files**: packs/*/

### 2026-06-19 — learn skill: Learning Record System (Option B)
**Decision**: The `learn` skill uses a dedicated `memory/learning-records/` cold tier of sequential `LR-NNNN-slug.md` files with frontmatter (name, date, type, status) and body sections (Insight, Evidence, Implications). Triggered both by the Supervisor inline and by the user via `/learn`. Superseded LRs are archived in-place (never deleted). Skill promotion fires only on ≥2 recurring LRs and requires explicit user approval before any SKILL.md is saved.
**Why**: Passive memory (only updated on git push) let mid-session insights evaporate. LR provenance + supersession chains prevent stale entries from silently poisoning future sub-agent context. Option A (inline enricher) lacked supersession; Option C (auto-skill generator) was premature and fragile.
**Files**: .claude/skills/learn/SKILL.md, templates/LEARNING-RECORD-FORMAT.md, setup.sh, CLAUDE.md

### 2026-06-19 — Dark neon theme applied to HTML report templates
**Decision**: Both `report_template.html` and `thinking_report_template.html` now use a dark neon visual theme: background `#0a0a12`, surface `#111128`, and a cyan/green/purple/amber/red neon accent palette. Gauge values have `text-shadow` glow; progress bars have `box-shadow` glow; badges use colored borders + glow.
**Why**: User requested the theme match the dark neon aesthetic of an inventory management dashboard (Taskade). Improves visual differentiation of severity/status at a glance and modernises the look of generated reports.
**Files**: templates/report_template.html, templates/thinking_report_template.html

### 2026-06-18 — reports/ folder is local-only, never committed
**Decision**: Generated HTML reports are saved to `reports/` and excluded from git via `.gitignore`. Reports are not attached to PRs or CI artifacts.
**Why**: User confirmed local browser viewing only. Committing binary-ish generated files adds noise to git history without value.
**Files**: .gitignore
**Amended 2026-07-19**: one exception carved out — `reports/token-audit_*.md` (the DDR-0001 Token Audit Log) IS tracked. Discovered live: each Stage-3 spawn runs in an isolated worktree, so a fully-gitignored `reports/` meant the audit log never propagated across spawns/worktrees, defeating its purpose as an accumulating cross-session instrument. `.gitignore` changed from `reports/` to `reports/*` + `!reports/token-audit_*.md` (the blanket form blocks git from evaluating negations on its contents — must exclude contents, not the directory, for a file-level exception to work). HTML reports remain fully local-only; only this one file pattern is the exception.
**Files**: .gitignore

### 2026-06-19 — wake skill: mandatory cold-start briefing (Option B — Live Skill)
**Decision**: The `wake` skill is a mandatory hard gate at the start of every new Supervisor session. It reads four live sources (git log, PROJECT_KANBAN.md, memory/MEMORY.md, active LRs) and emits a ≤50-line structured briefing inline. Placement in CLAUDE.md is before the 5-Stage Pipeline section so the Supervisor encounters it first. Language is mandatory ("must", "not optional", "Do not skip") — never a soft suggestion.
**Why**: Cold-start token waste: without wake, the Supervisor re-reads PROJECT_SPEC.md (~200+ lines), KANBAN (grows unbounded), all cold memory files, and git log just to orient itself. wake distils all of that to ≤50 lines, saving both tokens and time per session (user's explicit insight). Option A (snapshot file) was rejected because it rots on crash and is always one session behind. Option C (hook-driven) was rejected for fragile "first message" detection and silent failure risk.
**Files**: .claude/skills/wake/SKILL.md, CLAUDE.md, README.md, memory/MEMORY.md

### 2026-06-19 — teach + write-better-skill: two-skill craft system for skill authoring
**Decision**: Skill authoring is handled by two distinct skills. `write-better-skill` is a model-invoked pure-reference craft guide (ported from mattpocock's writing-great-skills, adapted with framework-specific sections: pipeline integration table, Karpathy alignment, project leading-word examples). `teach` is a model-invoked orchestrator that auto-fires on skill-writing requests, consults `write-better-skill` principles, and emits a ready-to-save SKILL.md draft as a fenced code block with a registration checklist.
**Why**: A single "do everything" skill violated single-responsibility and produced sprawl. Separating craft knowledge (write-better-skill) from orchestration (teach) keeps each skill prunable and lets write-better-skill be used standalone to audit existing skills. The two-skill split directly follows mattpocock's own principle: split by invocation when another skill must reach the reference.
**Files**: .claude/skills/teach/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/write-better-skill/SKILL.md, CLAUDE.md, memory/MEMORY.md

<!-- ### 2026-06-12 — Example entry
**Decision**: ...
**Why**: ...
**Files**: src/example.py -->

### 2026-06-22 — CLAUDE_LEGACY.md kept in sync with CLAUDE.md (manual diff-driven)
**Decision**: `CLAUDE_LEGACY.md` is a maintained sibling of `CLAUDE.md`. When new skills, session-startup rules, or stage workflow steps are added to CLAUDE.md, they must be mirrored into CLAUDE_LEGACY.md — specifically: the skills table in the Mandatory Folder Structure section, session-startup gates (e.g. `wake`), Stage 4 report invocations (`html-report` after each review skill), and Permanent Rules / Hard-Stop Gates. Version is bumped on each sync.
**Why**: Legacy projects run under CLAUDE_LEGACY.md. Without the sync, legacy sessions miss new skills and workflow steps — e.g. html-report was listed in the skills folder but never invoked in Stage 4.
**Files**: CLAUDE_LEGACY.md, CLAUDE.md

### 2026-06-22 — Hard-stop gates added to Permanent Rules (both CLAUDE.md and CLAUDE_LEGACY.md)
**Decision**: Four Supervisor-level hard-stop gates added that cannot be bypassed by any rationale: (1) No TASK_GUIDE = no work — Supervisor must never write implementation code directly; (2) Complexity floor C2/Medium Risk for any refactor/QA/restructure task; (3) KANBAN must be updated before session ends when work is completed; (4) One project per KANBAN — distinct tech scopes get separate boards.
**Why**: Pipeline compliance was being bypassed when tasks "felt small" (LR-0001, LR-0002). The existing Permanent Rules only gated sub-agents; the Supervisor itself had no explicit self-check rule blocking direct implementation. Hard-stop language makes the constraint visible at the rule level, not just in LR memory.
**Files**: CLAUDE.md, CLAUDE_LEGACY.md

### 2026-06-24 — Compound Engineering skill batch (6 net-new + 2 upgrades)
**Decision**: Added 8 skills sourced from compound-engineering-plugin analysis: `strategy` (STRATEGY.md north star), `ideate` (pre-brainstorm divergent filter), `resolve-pr-feedback` (post-Stage-4 PR thread resolution), `compound` (problem→solution capture to docs/solutions/), `compound-refresh` (docs/solutions/ drift audit), `optimize` (metric-driven iteration loop, optional). Upgraded `brainstorming` with scope tiers + one-question-per-turn + visual probe gate + claim verification. Created project-level `code-review` override adding P0–P3 severity, confidence anchors, cross-reviewer dedup/promotion, conditional personas, model tiering.
**Why**: Gap analysis vs. compound-engineering-plugin showed we lacked: a product north star artifact (STRATEGY.md), a pre-brainstorm idea filter (ideate), a PR feedback resolution loop (resolve-pr-feedback), a structured solutions library (compound/compound-refresh), and metric-driven iteration (optimize). The code-review upgrade adds structured finding quality gates we were missing. All skills adapted to our conventions: no TypeScript/GraphQL internals, decisions land in PROJECT_SPEC.md/memory/, headless mode maps to supervisor-invoked mode.
**Files**: .claude/skills/strategy/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/ideate/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/resolve-pr-feedback/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/compound/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/compound-refresh/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/optimize/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/code-review/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md, CLAUDE.md, memory/MEMORY.md

### 2026-06-24 — code-review project override replaces built-in for Stage 4
**Decision**: `.claude/skills/code-review/SKILL.md` is a project-level override of the built-in `code-review` skill. Claude Code resolves project skills before built-ins. The override adds: P0–P3 severity scale, 0/25/50/75/100 confidence anchors, confidence gate (suppress P1–P3 at <75), always-on reviewer personas (correctness, testing, maintainability, standards), conditional personas (security, perf, migration, adversarial, api), cross-reviewer dedup + promotion (+1 anchor when 2+ reviewers agree), model tiering (P0/security → top model; others → mid-tier).
**Why**: Built-in code-review produces a flat comment list with no severity or confidence metadata. The structured finding pipeline reduces noise (low-confidence findings suppressed), promotes signal (cross-reviewer agreement), and produces a diff that is demonstrably cleaner after safe fixes are auto-applied.
**Files**: .claude/skills/code-review/SKILL.md, CLAUDE.md

### 2026-06-24 — docs/solutions/ as the compound knowledge store
**Decision**: `docs/solutions/[category]/[slug].md` is the canonical location for problem→solution artifacts written by the `compound` skill and audited by `compound-refresh`. Separate from `memory/learning-records/` (session-scoped LRs written by `learn`). `docs/solutions/` is agent-discoverable: CLAUDE.md references it as a pre-implementation scan target per category.
**Why**: `learn` captures session insights (patterns, corrections, gotchas) into LRs. `compound` captures full problem→solution artifacts with structured frontmatter (track, category, tags, status) optimized for future-agent discovery. The two stores answer different questions: LRs answer "what did we learn?"; solutions answer "how do we solve [X] in this codebase?".
**Files**: .claude/skills/compound/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/compound-refresh/SKILL.md, CLAUDE.md

### 2026-06-24 — slim-skills: token reduction via behavioral-checksum-gated pruning
**Decision**: Add `slim-skills` skill to audit and prune SKILL.md files exceeding 150 lines. Pruning extracts a behavioral checksum (hard constraints, never-do rules, output assertions, completion criteria) before cutting, and verifies checksum survival after write. Human approval required via diff gate before any file is saved. Never auto-saves.
**Why**: Bloated SKILL.md files increase token cost on every invocation. The 150-line threshold was chosen based on the current skill distribution (two files exceed it: `learn` at 182 and `map-codebase` at 165). The checksum gate prevents silent behavioral regression — the primary risk of naive line-trimming. External compression tools (e.g. Headroom) were evaluated and rejected: they require MCP/network and the project constraint is local-only, documents/HTML only.
**Files**: .claude/skills/slim-skills/SKILL.md, CLAUDE.md, memory/MEMORY.md

### 2026-06-29 — bugfix skill: Supervisor-level bug fix orchestration
**Decision**: Add `bugfix` skill as the single entry point for all bug fixes — analogous to `/plan` for implementation. Flow: triage intake (symptom, trigger, severity) → **orient phase (hard gate)** → TASK_GUIDE → sub-agent spawn with `diagnose` wired in → Stage 4 review → integrate. The orient phase (Step 2) is the critical addition: read relevant code + git history + memory before forming any theory; force an explicit "what does correct look like?" from the user; write a 3–5 sentence mental model statement; user must confirm before TASK_GUIDE is written. Confirmed mental model is injected verbatim into the sub-agent spawn prompt. Sub-agent must stop and report if diagnosis contradicts the confirmed model. Fix code is blocked until all Diagnosis Gates are checked. P0 bugs floor at Medium Risk. Model-invoked.
**Why**: Wrong mental model at intake = wrong fix direction with no way back. Surface intake alone (symptom + repro steps) is insufficient — it captures what the user observes but not what the code is supposed to do or where the divergence actually occurs. The orient gate forces investigation before theory formation, and user confirmation locks the shared understanding before any sub-agent work begins.
**Files**: .claude/skills/bugfix/SKILL.md, CLAUDE.md, CLAUDE_LEGACY.md, memory/MEMORY.md

### 2026-07-01 — ui-test skill: self-degrading easy-ui-mcp orchestration (T015 + T016)
**Decision**: Add `ui-test` skill wrapping the external `easy-ui-mcp` (github.com/thunderkds/easy-ui-mcp) local Docker/MCP server for web UI testing. Health-checks `http://localhost:8765/health` first; if down, or the calling task has no UI/Design AC section, emits a one-line skip note and exits cleanly — never blocks the pipeline. When healthy, drives the 8 MCP tools in sequence (`ui_start_session` → `ui_navigate` → `ui_click`/`ui_fill` → `ui_assert` → `ui_get_page_state` → `ui_take_screenshot` → `ui_end_session`) and maps the output directly into TASK_GUIDE Evidence rows — the same run satisfies both Stage 4 Hard-Stop Gate 6 (UI Evidence) and the Stage 5 `verify` row. Selected over (a) a hard-wired mandatory Stage 4/5 gate (breaks on machines without Docker) and (b) folding MCP calls directly into `qa.md` with no skill file (breaks Skills-vs-Agents separation, not reusable). Chosen via `BRAINSTORMING_LOG.md` (approved 2026-07-01) as "Option B — Self-Degrading Optional Skill". Web-only in v1; mobile explicitly deferred. **T016 (done, 2026-07-01)** wired the skill in: `.claude/agents/qa.md` gained a `ui-test` row (invoked only when the TASK_GUIDE has a non-deleted UI/Design AC section); `CLAUDE.md` gained a skills-table row plus one added sentence on Hard-Stop Gate 6 clarifying that `ui-test`'s MCP evidence satisfies the three UI Evidence rows when available, without waiving the requirement when it isn't (manual/other verification remains acceptable).
**Why**: UI Evidence today is manual/observational. `easy-ui-mcp` gives real Chromium-backed verification, but it's local-only infra that may not be running on every machine — a hard gate would create pipeline friction for tasks that "feel small" (exactly the anti-pattern the Hard-Stop Gates otherwise guard against), so the skill must degrade gracefully rather than block.
**Files**: .claude/skills/ui-test/SKILL.md, .claude/agents/qa.md, CLAUDE.md, BRAINSTORMING_LOG.md, tasks/TASK_GUIDE_T015.md (local-only), tasks/TASK_GUIDE_T016.md (local-only)

### 2026-07-06 — Code Naming Conventions added to CLAUDE.md (code-level only, not skills/agents/infra)
**Decision**: Added a "Code Naming Conventions" section to CLAUDE.md (after Karpathy Engineering Principles), mandatory for all sub-agents (Backend, Frontend, Common-Infrastructure, QA) at Stage 4 code-review. Covers: functions/methods as verbs, boolean `is/has/can/should` prefixes, classes/interfaces/types as nouns, descriptive variables (no abbreviations), `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` constants, file-matches-export naming, REST endpoints as plural noun resources (verb lives in HTTP method), past-tense event names, `Error`/`Exception`-suffixed error classes, language-native private-member markers, behavior-stated test names, PascalCase frontend components + `use`-prefixed hooks, plural snake_case DB tables with singular non-repeated columns, prefixed `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` env vars, lowercase kebab/snake directories, PascalCase enum types, and `T`-style generic type parameters. Existing project/language linter conventions take precedence where they conflict (Surgical Changes principle).
**Why**: User explicitly scoped this to source-code naming only — distinct from the Stage 1.5/skills-vs-agents naming (skill/command/agent names were explicitly excluded). Table was built via an iterative gap-review pass (first draft covered 8 elements; user asked to review for completeness, which surfaced 11 more categories: errors, private members, tests, frontend components/hooks, DB, env vars, directories, enums, generics).
**Files**: CLAUDE.md
**Branch**: docs/code-naming-conventions — merged to main via PR #44

### 2026-07-07 — Deterministic guardrail hooks: step limit, event trace, verified merge gate
**Decision**: Added three hook-layer guardrails moving reliability enforcement from model self-report to harness code, per an external "deterministic harness" framework the user brought in (max-steps, verify-step, event-tracing, multi-attempt ideas — auth-handling and context-compaction ideas from the same framework were rejected as inapplicable/uncontrollable in this environment, see below). New: `pre_agent_step_limit.py` (PreToolUse, matcher `.*`) counts tool calls per Task ID (regex `T\d{3}` found anywhere in `tool_input`) into `.claude/hooks/.state/step_count_<task>.txt` and blocks once `CLAUDE_STEP_LIMIT` (default 40) is exceeded, telling the Supervisor to stop and escalate rather than let a stuck task loop. New: `post_tool_trace.py` (PostToolUse, matcher `.*`) appends `{timestamp, tool_name, summary, is_error}` for every tool call to `memory/event-trace/<task>.jsonl` (untagged calls go to `_untagged.jsonl`) — a ground-truth history the Verify step can inspect instead of trusting the model's claim. Modified `pre_bash_block_unsafe_merge.py`: the push/merge gate now requires **both** a filled verify Evidence row in the TASK_GUIDE **and** a matching non-error trace record containing a real test/verify command (`pytest|npm test|jest|go test|cargo test|verify`, see `TEST_COMMAND_PATTERN`) — fails closed if the trace file is missing or empty, closing the "agent lies about running tests" gap. Modified `post_agent_move_to_review.py` to delete the task's step counter once it reaches Ready for Review, so a rework cycle gets a fresh budget. Both new hooks registered in `.claude/settings.json`. Runtime state (`.claude/hooks/.state/`, `memory/event-trace/`) is gitignored — local-only, not committed.
**Why**: Two of the five framework ideas were explicitly rejected as not applicable here: (1) context-window compression — Claude Code manages its own context/auto-compaction; there is no hook point to override it. (2) programmatic login/auth handling — this harness orchestrates Claude Code sub-agents, not a browser/UI agent that hits login pages, so there's no credential/URL-state surface to intercept. The other three ideas mapped cleanly onto the existing hook architecture and closed a real gap: the prior verify gate (`pre_bash_block_unsafe_merge.py`) only regex-matched text in the TASK_GUIDE claiming "verify...pass" — it trusted the sub-agent's self-report with no independent evidence.
**Files**: .claude/hooks/pre_agent_step_limit.py (new), .claude/hooks/post_tool_trace.py (new), .claude/hooks/pre_bash_block_unsafe_merge.py, .claude/hooks/post_agent_move_to_review.py, .claude/settings.json, .gitignore
**Branch**: feat/deterministic-guardrails-hooks — merged to main via PR #45

### 2026-07-07 — T017: Task dependency declaration & entry-point reachability check (advisory, not a Hard-Stop Gate)
**Decision**: Closed a gap the user reported — Task B silently assuming Task A's output exists, and features implemented with no reachable entry point (dead code that passes its own tests). Resolved via `brainstorming` (Option C — Declarative + Verified Hybrid) then `grill-with-docs` (locked `Entry point` as canonical term over `Reachable via`; confirmed the new `Depends on` field stays separate from `PROJECT_KANBAN.md`'s existing `## Blocked` table, which remains a manual escape hatch for non-task/external blockers the hook can't verify). Implementation (T017, Done): `templates/TASK_GUIDE_template.md` gained a `## Dependencies & Reachability` section with `Depends on: Txxx` and `Entry point: [literal identifier]` fields (or `None`/`Standalone — N/A`). `.claude/hooks/pre_agent_validate_guide.py` now parses the target guide's `Depends on:` field at Agent-spawn time and emits a **non-blocking** `additionalContext` warning (never `"decision": "block"`) if the referenced task is unknown or not yet Done on `PROJECT_KANBAN.md` — verified via 4 synthetic stdin transcripts covering unknown-dep, done-dep (silent), not-done-dep, and no-field (silent) cases. `.claude/skills/code-review/SKILL.md` gained a Phase 0.5 "Entry-Point Reachability Check": greps the diff/repo for a task's declared `Entry point` literal string and raises a P2 finding (not a hard block) if absent. `CLAUDE.md` documents both fields under Stage 2 planning and explicitly states this is advisory, not a 7th Hard-Stop Gate. `templates/PROJECT_KANBAN_template.md` got a one-line legend note pointing to where `Depends on` actually lives (the TASK_GUIDE, not the board).
**Why**: Same shape as the guardrail-hooks work — declare the claim where the Supervisor already has context (Stage 2), verify a cheap, checkable form of it at an existing gate (spawn-time hook, code-review), fail toward a warning not a block. Circular-dependency detection and multi-dependency parsing (only the first `T\d{3}` in the `Depends on:` line is checked) were explicitly scoped out as single-hop-only for v1 — noted as a documented limitation, not a bug (see `code-review` P3 findings for T017).
**Files**: templates/TASK_GUIDE_template.md, templates/PROJECT_KANBAN_template.md, .claude/hooks/pre_agent_validate_guide.py, .claude/skills/code-review/SKILL.md, CLAUDE.md, PROJECT_SPEC.md (glossary), tasks/TASK_GUIDE_T017.md (now tracked — see 2026-07-14 gitignore decision below; was gitignored at time of this entry)
**Branch**: feat/task-dependency-tracking — merged to main via PR #47 (2026-07-07)

### 2026-07-14 — T018/T019/T020: Kanban auto-registration bugfix trio + live-instance gitignore
**Decision**: Fixed three bugs found in a repo scan. (1) T018: `.claude/hooks/post_write_register_task.py`'s title-extraction regex failed on real guide headings twice over — first because the character class between `TASK_GUIDE` and `T\d+` didn't accept the em-dash separator used in actual headings (`# TASK_GUIDE — T005: ...`), and after that fix landed, code-review (Stage 4) caught a second, deeper bug: `re.search(pattern, guide, re.IGNORECASE)` had no `re.MULTILINE`, so `^`/`$` anchored to the whole (multi-line) file string instead of the first line — the fix passed its own regression tests (which only used single-line strings) but still returned `"untitled"` against real files. Added `re.MULTILINE` and a regression test that uses realistic multi-line file content, not just isolated strings. (2) T019: reconciled `PROJECT_KANBAN.md` — corrected T005–T012 rows from the `"untitled"` fallback to their real title/agent/complexity/risk/priority, and removed task 013/task 014 rows entirely since no backing `tasks/TASK_GUIDE_*.md` exists for either (Hard-Stop Gate 1: no TASK_GUIDE = no work). (3) T020: `.gitignore` previously ignored `tasks/*`, `memory/*`, `PROJECT_SPEC.md`, `PROJECT_KANBAN.md`, `BRAINSTORMING_LOG.md`, `PRD.md` under a "framework distribution, never commit" framing, but this repo is used as a live dogfood instance and several of those files (`memory/MEMORY.md`, `tasks/TASK_GUIDE_T001_EXAMPLE.md`, `PROJECT_SPEC.md`, `PROJECT_KANBAN.md`) were already tracked in git despite being gitignored. User confirmed the live-instance framing; `.gitignore` Section 1 was rewritten to stop ignoring these categories, while still ignoring genuinely ephemeral paths (`.claude/hooks/.state/`, `memory/event-trace/`) and `reports/` (regenerable generated output — unchanged from the 2026-06-18 decision above).
**Why**: A wrong mental model that "isolated unit tests passing" implies "the real code path works" produced a fix that only worked in the test harness, not in production — code-review's job is exactly to catch that gap before merge, and it did. The Kanban/gitignore drift had compounded silently (every new TASK_GUIDE write since the hook was introduced registered as `"untitled"`) until a full repo scan surfaced it.
**Files**: .claude/hooks/post_write_register_task.py, .claude/hooks/tests/test_post_write_register_task.py (new), PROJECT_KANBAN.md, .gitignore, tasks/TASK_GUIDE_T018.md, tasks/TASK_GUIDE_T019.md, tasks/TASK_GUIDE_T020.md

### 2026-07-14 — T021/T022/T023: craft-spawn-prompt skill + hardened spawn-hook + memory cleanup (brainstorming Option B)
**Decision**: A brainstorming pass (`BRAINSTORMING_LOG.md`) on "auto-generate sub-agent spawn prompts" surfaced a live landmine: `.claude/hooks/pre_agent_validate_guide.py` hard-blocks any `Agent()` spawn on any bare `T\d+` token in the prompt with no backing `tasks/TASK_GUIDE_Txxx.md` — and `memory/MEMORY.md`/`decisions.md` (mandatorily pasted into every spawn prompt) already contained dangling `T013`/`T014` prose mentions from the T019 cleanup. User chose Option B (root-cause hook fix) over the initially-recommended Option D (defensive pre-flight-only, hook untouched). Landed as three tasks: (T021) reworded the dangling `T013`/`T014` mentions in `MEMORY.md`/`decisions.md` so they no longer match a bare-`T\d+` scan. (T022) added `extract_structural_task_ids()` to `pre_agent_validate_guide.py`, replacing the old `re.findall(r"\bT(\d+)\b", prompt)` blocking-extraction with a narrower one matching only two structural markers — a literal `TASK_GUIDE_Txxx.md` file-path reference, or an explicit `Task ID:`/`**Task ID**:` declaration line — so free prose mentions (decision-log sentences, etc.) never trigger the hard block, while `check_dependency_warnings()`'s separate `**Depends on**:` parsing (the advisory, non-blocking T017 mechanism) was left untouched; verified with 12 tests including real-file-content cases (avoiding the T018 "passed synthetic strings, failed on real data" trap). (T023) added `.claude/skills/craft-spawn-prompt/SKILL.md` — reads a TASK_GUIDE + agent guide + `MEMORY.md`, auto-detects standard vs. bugfix-flavored shape (via a `### Mental Model` heading), assembles the proven 5-element prompt, pre-flight-checks it against T022's exact hardened pattern, recommends a spawn model by Complexity, and outputs a fenced prompt block — never calls `Agent` itself. `CLAUDE.md` Stage 3 and `bugfix` Step 4 both now invoke this skill instead of restating the checklist inline, closing the two-call-site-drift risk that motivated the whole request.
**Why**: The Supervisor was hand-assembling spawn prompts inconsistently across Stage 3 and `bugfix` (proven during T018–T020's own execution). A naive "just add a prompt-crafting skill" (Option A) would have shipped broken — blocked by the pre-existing MEMORY.md landmine on its very first real use. Root-causing the hook (Option B, user's explicit choice) closes the false-positive class permanently rather than requiring perpetual per-prompt discipline.
**Files**: .claude/hooks/pre_agent_validate_guide.py, .claude/hooks/tests/test_pre_agent_validate_guide.py (new), .claude/skills/craft-spawn-prompt/SKILL.md (new), CLAUDE.md, .claude/skills/bugfix/SKILL.md, memory/MEMORY.md, memory/decisions.md, tasks/TASK_GUIDE_T021.md, tasks/TASK_GUIDE_T022.md, tasks/TASK_GUIDE_T023.md
**Follow-up flagged**: T024 (Todo) — `post_write_register_task.py`'s `agent`-field regex has the same boundary-matching bug class, confirmed live during T021/T022/T023's own Kanban auto-registration (matched "Agent guide" instead of "Assigned agent", registering `"guide"` as the agent).

### 2026-07-16 — T025: craft-agent skill (optional, supplemental agent-drafting)
**Decision**: Added `.claude/skills/craft-agent/SKILL.md`, a structural clone of `teach` (draft-only output, Ask-vs-Guess prerequisite gate, Registration checklist) but targeting `.claude/agents/*.md` instead of `SKILL.md`. Explored via `brainstorming` (`BRAINSTORMING_LOG.md`, Option A — Simple Path, over a rejected shared-craft-lib abstraction and a rejected "overload teach" minimalist path) and sharpened via `grill-with-docs` terminology mode (no `PRD.md` exists for this internal task, same precedent as T023) — locked two new canonical `PROJECT_SPEC.md` glossary terms: **Base team** (renamed from CLAUDE.md's prior "default core team" wording — the 4 agents every project starts with unconditionally) and **Agent Draft** (the fenced `.claude/agents/<name>.md` block the skill emits, never written to disk directly). Mid-session the user corrected the initial framing: `craft-agent` must be optional and invoked only when a requirement implies a role the base team doesn't cover — never a required Stage 1.5 step, since the base team already exists and covers most projects as-is. `craft-agent` operates in **whole-team mode** (one invocation proposes the full supplemental roster, not one role per call) and every Agent Draft states explicit inheritance from `general-agent-template.md`, listing only overrides. `CLAUDE.md` Stage 1.5 was updated to state the base team is unconditional and `craft-agent` fires only conditionally.
Stage 4 code-review (`reports/code-review_feat-craft-agent-skill_20260716T010000.html`) caught two findings, both fixed before merge: (P1) the skill's Registration checklist pointed to a nonexistent "CLAUDE.md Stage 1.5 sub-agent team table" — the real per-project table lives in `PROJECT_SPEC.md`'s `## Sub-Agent Team` section; (P2) `CLAUDE.md` line 381 used both "default core team" and "base team" in the same sentence, left over from the diff not fully adopting the session's own terminology decision — collapsed to the single canonical term.
**Why**: Mirrors the `teach`/SKILL.md gap `craft-agent` closes for `.claude/agents/*.md` — but scoped strictly supplemental per explicit user correction, avoiding the false implication that agents don't exist until generated (Option B's shared craft-lib was rejected as speculative — no second consumer exists yet; Option C's teach-overload was rejected for blurring `teach`'s own trigger).
**Files**: .claude/skills/craft-agent/SKILL.md (new), CLAUDE.md (custom-skill table row + Stage 1.5 section), PROJECT_SPEC.md (glossary: Base team, Agent Draft, craft-agent), tasks/TASK_GUIDE_T025.md, PROJECT_KANBAN.md
**Branch**: feat/craft-agent-skill — merged to main, pushed and merged via PR #48
**Follow-up flagged**: T026 (Todo) — `templates/TASK_GUIDE_template.md`'s own example `verify` Evidence row doesn't match `pre_bash_block_unsafe_merge.py`'s gate regex (see `memory/learnings.md` 2026-07-16 gotcha), discovered live blocking T025's merge twice. Fix the template's example row so future tasks don't copy a self-defeating format.

### 2026-07-16 — T027: DDR (Design Decision Record) — default decision artifact, ADR as rare escalation
**Decision**: Added `templates/DDR_template.md`, a structural clone of `templates/ADR_template.md` (identical section order: Context/Decision/Alternatives Considered/Consequences) but write-gated at **2-of-3** of the ADR criteria (hard to reverse / surprising without context / genuine trade-off) instead of ADR's 3-of-3. Files live at `docs/ddr/NNNN-title.md`, referenced as `DDR-NNNN` (numbering independent of `docs/adr/`'s `ADR-NNNN` sequence — always prefix-disambiguated, never a bare number). Explored via `brainstorming` (`BRAINSTORMING_LOG.md`, Option A — Simple Path, over a rejected index-skill scalable path and a rejected in-file-heading minimalist path the user explicitly overruled) and sharpened via `grill-with-docs` terminology mode (no `PRD.md` for this internal task, same precedent as T023/T025) — locked three new `PROJECT_SPEC.md` glossary terms: **DDR**, **ADR** (redefined as the rare escalation, not the only standalone option), and **decisions.md entry** (the lowest, always-written tier). `.claude/skills/grill-with-docs/SKILL.md`'s "Where decisions land" section now checks the DDR gate first; if a decision also clears all 3 ADR criteria, it is flagged ADR-eligible and the user is asked before escalating — DDR is never silently auto-upgraded. If fewer than 2 criteria hold, the decision stays a `memory/decisions.md`-only entry (no DDR/ADR offered). `CLAUDE.md`'s folder-structure requirements list and Step 1.5 Ambiguity Resolution Protocol sentence were updated to reference DDR as the default, ADR as the rare escalation.
Stage 4 code-review (`reports/code-review_feat-ddr-decision-record_20260716T030000.html`) caught one finding, fixed before merge: (P2) `grill-with-docs`'s `Output` line was updated to say "DDRs/ADRs" but its `Default Notification` line one section below still said only "K ADRs recorded" — collapsed to count both tiers.
**Supervisor process note**: the implementing agent left its `templates/DDR_template.md` and `CLAUDE.md` changes **uncommitted** in the worktree (only reported them via text); the Supervisor's first merge attempt only picked up the Stage-4 review-fix commit (which happened to touch a different file, `grill-with-docs/SKILL.md`), producing an incomplete merge to `main` missing the DDR template and CLAUDE.md wiring entirely. Caught by checking `git diff main --stat` output line-by-line against the expected 3-file scope rather than trusting the merge command's success. Fixed by committing the worktree's real uncommitted changes, then `git reset --hard` on the still-unpushed local `main` merge commit and re-merging cleanly — safe only because nothing had been pushed yet (verified via `git log github/main -1` before resetting).
**Why**: ADR's 3-of-3 gate is correctly rare (verified zero ADRs exist despite 15+ decisions logged this repo's life) but leaves no standalone, addressable record for the design decisions this project actually produces (e.g. T017, T021-023, T025 itself). DDR reuses ADR's proven per-file shape at a lower, more frequently-met bar without loosening ADR's own gate.
**Files**: templates/DDR_template.md (new), .claude/skills/grill-with-docs/SKILL.md ("Where decisions land" section), CLAUDE.md (folder-structure list + Step 1.5 sentence), PROJECT_SPEC.md (glossary: DDR, ADR, decisions.md entry), tasks/TASK_GUIDE_T027.md, PROJECT_KANBAN.md
**Branch**: feat/ddr-decision-record — merged to main (local merge, not yet pushed)

### 2026-07-17 — Token-efficiency refactor: measure first (Option B), slim-skills parallel (Option C), CLAUDE.md trim deferred → see DDR-0001
**Decision**: Before any token-cost refactor, run a baseline **Token Audit Log** (`reports/token-audit_<window>.md` — deliberately in `reports/`, not `memory/`, to keep the Memory Write Protocol's file set clean) over a **Measurement Window** of 7 sessions or 14 days (whichever first; session = one conversation that ran `wake`). Entries tagged by Task ID/`overhead`, cache-hit vs. miss, and model tier; session `/cost` logged as ground truth. `slim-skills` runs in parallel on the 4 skills >150 lines (`learn`, `map-codebase`, `bugfix`, `code-review`) — safe rider, invocation-triggered cost doesn't contaminate always-on measurement. Trimming `CLAUDE.md` (580 lines, every-turn injection, contains the Hard-Stop Gates) is explicitly **deferred** until data shows cold-start cost dominates. Success: ≥20% reduction in $ per completed task at same C-level; rollback: <5%. First-ever DDR written (`docs/ddr/0001-measure-first-token-refactor.md`), exercising the T027 pipeline end-to-end. Four glossary terms locked in `PROJECT_SPEC.md`: Token Audit Log, Measurement Window, Cold-start cost, $ per completed task.
**Why**: Spend concentration is unknown (user-confirmed); optimizing the biggest *file* is not the same as optimizing the biggest *cost* — Ask vs. Guess applied to billing. → see DDR-0001
**Files**: docs/ddr/0001-measure-first-token-refactor.md (new), PROJECT_SPEC.md (glossary ×4), BRAINSTORMING_LOG.md, memory/decisions.md
**Follow-up**: Stage 2 `/plan` — TASK_GUIDEs for the audit convention + slim-skills run; post-window follow-up brainstorm against real numbers.

**T028 completed (2026-07-17)**: `reports/token-audit_2026-07-17.md` scaffold (entry format, window-close condition, 3 sample entries) + `memory/MEMORY.md` +1 hot-tier reminder line + `.claude/hooks/tests/test_token_audit_format.py` (5 tests, all passing: scaffold exists, header documents required elements, sample entries match the entry regex, malformed/missing-task-tag entry is rejected, MEMORY.md ≤200 lines). Branch `feat/token-audit-log` pushed to github (commit 43f465c) — not yet merged, Stage 4 review pending. Baseline Measurement Window is now open as of this commit's date.
**Follow-up flagged**: the spawned sub-agent produced correct, test-passing artifacts but never committed or reported back — the Ghostty terminal window closed (or the process ended) before the completion marker was written, and the background wait-loop task itself was later reported `stopped` with no completion record. Supervisor found and committed the finished work manually after verifying tests passed. This is the same failure shape as T027's near-miss (uncommitted worktree work almost lost at merge time) — second occurrence. Consider whether the Ghostty spawn pattern needs a more durable completion signal than a single marker-file wait-loop (e.g. periodic Supervisor-side polling of `git status` in the worktree, not just the marker).

**T029 completed (2026-07-17)**: `/slim-skills` run (HITL, inline in Supervisor session — no worktree/spawn) on the 4 candidates >150 lines. Results: `learn` 182→128 (−30%, approved), `map-codebase` 165→130 (−21%, approved — safe floor, the exact shell commands + markdown template are checksum items), `bugfix` 160→160 (skipped — only 2% trimmable, the embedded TASK_GUIDE template block + 6 completion-criterion lines dominate the file and are all checksum items), `code-review` 157→146 (−7%, approved). Every approved file passed Step 5 checksum verification (all hard constraints, thresholds, and notification strings present verbatim; zero reverts needed). Net: 664→564 lines across the 4 files (−15% combined), 2 genuinely pruned, 2 confirmed near their behavioral floor rather than actually bloated.
**Learning**: line count alone is a weak bloat signal for this project's skills — files dominated by exact shell commands, embedded templates, or numeric gate tables (map-codebase, bugfix, code-review) have little compressible prose regardless of total length, while files that are mostly explanatory narrative (learn) compress readily. The `slim-skills` 150-line threshold is a reasonable *candidate* filter but not a reliable *bloat* predictor on its own.
### 2026-07-17 — Fidelity Gate: hallucination check for generated skills/agents lives in write-better-skill (Option A)
**Decision**: `teach` and `craft-agent` had no check that a drafted SKILL.md/agent .md actually traces to the requirement it was drafted for — a drafted artifact could hallucinate scope, reference a nonexistent `Skill()`/`Agent()`, or claim authority Permanent Rules reserve elsewhere (e.g. `memory/` writes), and unlike a bad code change it persists silently across every future session. Added one "Fidelity Gate (Hallucination Check)" reference section to `write-better-skill` (single source of truth, already consulted by both drafting skills) checking: (1) traceability to `PRD.md`/`PROJECT_SPEC.md`/user's literal words this session, (2) `Skill()`/`Agent()` reference resolution — unresolved refs flagged inline as companion-draft dependencies, draft still emits, (3) scope containment against Permanent Rules. `teach` and `craft-agent` each gained one workflow step before Emit that points to the gate. Explored via `brainstorming` (`BRAINSTORMING_LOG_fidelity-gate.md` — Option A over a rejected duplicated-checklist path and a rejected standalone-audit-skill path) and sharpened via `grill-with-docs` terminology mode. DDR gate checked: only 1-of-3 criteria hold (genuine trade-off among A/B/C; not hard to reverse — plain text edit; not surprising without context) — below the 2-of-3 threshold, so this stays a `decisions.md`-only entry, no DDR/ADR.
**Why**: user explicitly rejected a separate audit pass as too easy to skip — the check had to live inside the creation steps (`teach`/`craft-agent`'s own Emit sequence), not bolted on afterward.
**Files**: .claude/skills/write-better-skill/SKILL.md (new "Fidelity Gate" section), .claude/skills/teach/SKILL.md (new pre-Emit step), .claude/skills/craft-agent/SKILL.md (new pre-Emit step), PROJECT_SPEC.md (glossary: Fidelity Gate), BRAINSTORMING_LOG_fidelity-gate.md (new)

### 2026-07-17 — Direct-to-repo install/update, no central clone → see ADR-0001
**Decision**: Replaced the `~/.supervisor` central-clone + symlink model with temp-clone-copy-discard: `setup.sh`/`update.sh` `git clone --depth 1` into a `mktemp -d`, copy `MANIFEST` paths + `CLAUDE.md` as real files into the working repo, discard the temp clone. `setup.sh` always full-overwrites (fresh install, no conflict logic). New `update.sh` (separate script, not a `setup.sh` flag — shares fetch logic via new `lib/harness-fetch.sh`) tracks installed-file hashes in a new git-tracked `.claude/harness-lock.json`; on update, untouched files overwrite silently, customized files (hash mismatch) get a diff + per-file prompt (overwrite/skip/view). New prerequisite: both scripts require the working directory to be a real git repo (git is now the only undo mechanism, no symlink safety net). Packs (`packs/`, `install_pack()`) explicitly out of scope — keep current symlink model until a follow-up. Migration for existing symlink-based installs also deferred — `update.sh` refuses (doesn't silently convert) if it finds a symlink at a `MANIFEST` path. First-ever ADR written (`docs/adr/0001-direct-repo-install-no-central-clone.md`) — cleared all 3 ADR criteria (hard to reverse for existing installs, surprising vs. documented model, genuine trade-off among 3 fetch mechanisms), escalated from DDR by user choice. `PROJECT_SPEC.md` glossary updated: Central clone marked superseded, General resources redefined, two new terms (Temp-clone-copy-discard, harness-lock.json).
**Why**: user wants each project fully self-contained, zero dependency on shared local state; the symlink model's "edit once, every project picks it up" convenience was explicitly traded away for install independence.
**Files**: docs/adr/0001-direct-repo-install-no-central-clone.md (new), PROJECT_SPEC.md (glossary), BRAINSTORMING_LOG_direct-install.md (new)
**Follow-up**: Stage 2 `/plan` for lib/harness-fetch.sh + setup.sh rewrite + update.sh + harness-lock.json shape (C2/Medium floor — Hard-Stop Gate 2, "restructure"); deferred: symlink→direct migration path, packs model revisit, README update.

**T031 merged** (2026-07-17, `feat/harness-fetch-lib`): `lib/harness-fetch.sh` — `harness_make_temp_dir`, `harness_fetch`, `harness_copy_manifest`, cleanup traps. Stage 4 review: 0 P0/P1, 2 P2 (stderr swallowed on clone failure — deferred, low priority; whole-directory vs. per-file granularity — resolved by redirecting T033 to enumerate files itself rather than extending T031). Independent `verify` pass (not the author's own tests) confirmed overwrite semantics and a leading-slash MANIFEST-entry probe stays contained. See gotchas in `learnings.md` (stdout-vs-variable trap-registration bug class; no `shellcheck` in this env).

**T032 merged** (2026-07-17, worktree `agent-a60c669423604cff2` — note: the sub-agent's own `isolation: "worktree"` created a *second*, separate worktree/branch instead of reusing the one `common-infrastructure` had already set up at `pac-T032`/`feat/setup-direct-install`; that worktree/branch was orphaned and cleaned up unused): `setup.sh` rewritten — sources `lib/harness-fetch.sh`, `check_target_is_git_repo()` runs before any write, always-overwrite real copies via `harness_copy_manifest`, new `.claude/harness-lock.json` writer with **per-file** (not per-directory) content hashes, sha256/shasum content-only (chmod-independent). Stage 4 review: 0 P0/P1, 2 P2 accepted as-is (JSON path-key escaping doesn't cover all control chars — low risk, repo-controlled paths; `resolve_repo_url` gained a `SUPERVISOR_REPO` override for offline test fixtures, a deliberate deviation from the guide's "keep unchanged" note, accepted since additive-only). Independent `verify` (not the author's `tests/test_setup.sh`) confirmed real end-to-end behavior plus a probe not in the test suite: a stale symlink at a MANIFEST path (old-model leftover) is correctly overwritten to a real directory by `setup.sh` without touching the symlink's former target — confirms `setup.sh`'s "always overwrite" contract is distinct from `update.sh`'s (T033) "refuse on symlink" contract, not a contradiction between them. `install_pack()`/`packs/` confirmed untouched.

**T033 merged** (2026-07-17, `feat/update-sh-conflict-prompt`, spawned correctly this time without `isolation: "worktree"` against the pre-made worktree): `update.sh` fully rewritten — no more central-clone `git pull`. Re-fetches via `harness_fetch` only (NOT `harness_copy_manifest`, per T031's granularity resolution); `detect_symlinks()` refuses all-or-nothing on any old-model symlink at a MANIFEST path (no auto-convert); enumerates individual files itself (`build_fresh_file_list`) and per file either silently overwrites (hash still matches `.claude/harness-lock.json`) or shows a real diff and prompts `[o]verwrite/[s]kip/[v]iew` (reads fd 0 for the prompt, fd 3 for the file-list loop, so piped test input and a real TTY both work); on stdin EOF it does not guess — skips and exits 2 instructing an interactive re-run. Lock is re-recorded reflecting exactly what was accepted; removed-upstream files are preserved with a warning, never deleted. Stage 4 review: 0 P0/P1, 2 P2 accepted as-is (`detect_symlinks` runs after the fetch — no correctness impact, just a wasted clone on the refusal path; same JSON-escaping limitation as T031/T032). Independent `verify` drove 3 scenarios beyond the author's 22-assertion suite: view-diff-then-overwrite, and two simultaneous conflicts in one run (skip one, overwrite the other) — both resolved correctly and independently. Caught and self-corrected a shell-scoping mistake in the reviewer's *own* test command (`VAR=val cmd1 | cmd2` only scopes `VAR` to `cmd1`) — flagged as a footgun worth avoiding in T035's README examples. Second occurrence of the step-limit-hook false-positive (see `learnings.md`) — counter accumulates across sub-agent execution *and* Supervisor review, doesn't reset on "Ready for Review."

**T034 merged** (2026-07-19, `feat/install-update-qa-smoke`, real work recovered from a worktree that predated a failed `/compact`): independent QA smoke suite (`tests/test_install_update_smoke.sh`) exercising `setup.sh`+`update.sh` together end-to-end — fresh install (real files, no symlinks), silent no-op update, conflict prompt (both overwrite and skip), non-git-dir rejection for both scripts. Re-verified independently after the /compact gap: re-ran the suite (9/9), re-ran all three implementer unit suites (`test_harness_fetch.sh` 9/9, `test_setup.sh` 15/15, `test_update.sh` 22/22 — 55/55 total), and fixed one thing the original worktree session got wrong before the gap: its Evidence table's `verify` row still used the pre-T026-fix Check-cell format (extra text before the pipe), which would have failed the real merge gate — corrected to match T026's fixed convention (literal `verify` + "pass" in the Notes column) and re-verified against the live gate regex before merging. Includes a genuine negative-control self-check (sabotaged `update.sh`'s conflict-warning line, confirmed the suite goes red, reverted) — proves the oracle isn't a rubber stamp.
**T035 merged** (2026-07-19, direct commit to main, not a worktree): README.md install/update sections rewritten for the direct-repo model. **Notable: an earlier uncommitted working-tree edit to this exact TASK_GUIDE falsely claimed the work was already done** (Evidence checkmarks with descriptive "8 intentional pack-only hits" analysis) — a live `grep` against the actual `README.md` showed 9+ core-install-level stale symlink/central-clone references still present, contradicting the claim entirely. Discarded via `git checkout --` and redone for real, cross-checked line-by-line against `setup.sh`/`update.sh`'s own header comments (verified authoritative). Found and fixed two gaps beyond the original guide's scope: (1) packs still require a pre-existing `~/.supervisor` clone that the new `setup.sh` no longer auto-creates — added a note; (2) an obsolete "git submodule alternative" doc block no longer applies to the new model — removed rather than left stale.

**T036 merged** (2026-07-19, direct commit to main): `scripts/smoke-install.sh` — the script wired into `.github/workflows/ci.yml`'s "Install smoke test" step — was still written for the pre-ADR-0001 symlink/central-clone model and had never been updated across T031-T033's rewrite. **CI has been red on every push/PR to `main` since T031-T033 landed (2026-07-17), undetected until a user-requested consistency review.** Root cause: it copied only `setup.sh` itself into a scratch target (missing the co-located `lib/harness-fetch.sh` the new `setup.sh` sources relative to its own path) and relied on `SUPERVISOR_PATH` pointing at the local checkout, which the rewritten `setup.sh` no longer reads from for the core install (that variable is packs-only now). Fixed by invoking `setup.sh` from its real path (never copied) with `SUPERVISOR_REPO="file://$ROOT"` — the same offline `file://` fetch pattern already proven in `tests/test_setup.sh`/`tests/test_update.sh`/`tests/test_install_update_smoke.sh` — so it clones the actual current repo state rather than a synthetic fixture. Stage 4 self-review caught 1 P1: the "no central-clone directory created" assertion was checking a sentinel path that `SUPERVISOR_PATH` was never actually pointed at, making it trivially always-pass regardless of real behavior — fixed by wiring `SUPERVISOR_PATH` to the sentinel, mirroring `tests/test_setup.sh`'s `NO_CLONE` pattern. Verified live: reproduced the original failure first, confirmed the fix resolves it, ran the negative case (broken `SUPERVISOR_REPO` → exit 1, zero artifacts), and re-ran all 4 existing shell suites + the pytest hook suite for regression (79/79 total).
**Why**: A CI badge nobody checks is worse than no CI — this had been silently failing for 3 days across 5+ merged PRs before being caught. General lesson: any infra migration (like ADR-0001) needs "update every CI/test entry point" as an explicit checklist item, not an assumption that existing green checks stay green.
**Files**: scripts/smoke-install.sh, tasks/TASK_GUIDE_T036.md

**T037 merged** (2026-07-19, direct commit to main): CI's separate "Shellcheck install scripts" step failed immediately after T036's fix landed — reported live by the user as `SC1091 (info): Not following: lib/harness-fetch.sh was not specified as input (see shellcheck -x)` on both `setup.sh` and `update.sh`. Root cause: both files already carry the correct `# shellcheck source=lib/harness-fetch.sh` directive (added at T031), but `.github/workflows/ci.yml`'s `shellcheck` invocation never passed `-x` (`--external-sources`), so shellcheck recognized the annotated path but refused to actually follow and analyze it — and shellcheck exits non-zero on any finding, including "info," with no severity filter set, so this failed the whole CI step. Fixed by adding `-x` to the one-line invocation. `lib/harness-fetch.sh` gets real shellcheck coverage for the first time as a side effect (previously only indirectly linted via the two files that source it) — manually reviewed for common anti-patterns as a substitute (shellcheck unavailable in this dev env, same gap as T031/T036); found clean.
**Why**: A `source=` directive alone tells shellcheck *where* a dynamically-resolved sourced file is; it does not by itself enable following it — that's what `-x` is for. Two-part CI outage in one day (T036 + T037) underscores that the ADR-0001 migration touched more CI surface than the "update every entry point" lesson from T036 alone anticipated — worth a final CI green-run confirmation before considering the migration's CI story closed.
**Files**: .github/workflows/ci.yml, tasks/TASK_GUIDE_T037.md

**T038 merged** (2026-07-19, direct commit to main): User reported a live failure running the primary documented install command (`curl -fsSL .../setup.sh | sh`) against a real new project — `Required library not found: <cwd>/lib/harness-fetch.sh` followed by `curl: (23) Failure writing output to destination`. Root cause: `SCRIPT_DIR=$(dirname -- "$0")` resolves correctly only when `setup.sh` is a real file on disk; when piped via `curl | sh`, `$0` has no meaningful file location, so `SCRIPT_DIR` silently fell back to the caller's own cwd — never anywhere `lib/harness-fetch.sh` (T031) exists. Since T031 split fetch logic out of `setup.sh` into that separately-sourced library, the **primary documented install method** — step 1 of README's own Quick Start, re-verified as correct prose in T035 — has been completely broken for every new user since 2026-07-17, undetected by any of T031-T037's testing (all used real local checkouts, never a genuine piped invocation).
User approved (via `AskUserQuestion`) the "self re-exec via fresh clone" fix over two alternatives (inline-duplicate the fetch logic; drop `curl|sh` support entirely): when the co-located library is missing, `setup.sh` now does a plain `git clone --depth 1` (not `harness_fetch` — that library isn't sourced yet) into a temp dir, then re-invokes the real cloned `setup.sh` with original args passed through, then cleans up and propagates the real exit code. Self-review caught a genuine bug *before any test ran*: under `set -e`, `cmd; rc=$?` silently skips both the exit-code capture and the `rm -rf` cleanup if `cmd` fails, since the script exits immediately at the failing line — fixed to the `cmd || rc=$?` guard. Verified across 4 live scenarios: simulated piped install, `--pack=mobile` argument passthrough, negative case (broken bootstrap URL, zero artifacts/zero leftover temp dirs), and — the authoritative proof — the REAL `curl | sh` command run against the just-pushed `main`, exact reproduction of the user's original failure, now exit 0. `update.sh` confirmed out of scope (never piped in any documented usage).
**Why**: A one-line copy-paste install command (matching rustup/nvm/Homebrew UX) is the whole point of README's Quick Start section — silently breaking it while every other test stayed green is the same "checkmarks are claims, not facts" lesson from earlier in this session, just discovered by a real user instead of by review. General lesson: any change that splits monolithic script logic into a sourced library (T031's `lib/harness-fetch.sh`) must explicitly test the piped/`$0`-is-meaningless invocation path, not just checkout-based paths — the two have different `$0` semantics that only piped testing exposes.
**Files**: setup.sh, tasks/TASK_GUIDE_T038.md

### 2026-07-21 — T042: post_write_register_task.py metadata extraction — 4th regex defect in this hook family

**T042 merged** (2026-07-21, branch `docs/stage2-t039-t042`, commit `e35f987`): The Complexity/Risk/Priority regexes (`Complexity[:\s]+(C[0-3])` etc.) could not match the format `templates/TASK_GUIDE_template.md` itself produces (`**Complexity Level**: C2`) — `[:\s]+` cannot cross the intervening ` Level**: `. All three silently fell through to plausible-looking defaults (`C1`/`Low`/`P1`), so **the hook had never once correctly read those fields from a guide written to its own template**, and the defaults read as data. Discovered live: T039/T041 (both C2/Medium) auto-registered as C1/Low. Fixed by widening to tolerate the optional label word and markdown emphasis while still matching the bare `Complexity: C3` form (explicit anti-regression AC — the fix must widen, not swap one rigid format for another), and by changing all three defaults to `?` so a genuinely missing field is visibly unknown. Risk label canonicalised `Med` → `Medium`; verified by grep that the hook's own normaliser line was the only consumer of the old spelling.

**Why**: Risk gates `security-review` and Complexity carries the Hard-Stop Gate 2 floor, so a board understating either understates the process a task requires — a correctness bug in the *governance* layer, not just cosmetics. This is the **fourth** defect of the same shape in these hooks (T018 title regex, T022 task-ID extraction, T024 agent-field regex, now T042). The implementing agent flagged that a shared `extract_labeled_field()` helper would likely prevent a fifth; not built here (out of scope), worth a follow-up task.

**Stage 4 outcome**: 0 P0, 0 P1, 1 P2 (AC5 `Med`→`Medium` normaliser had no direct test — every fixture already wrote `Medium` in full; sent back and fixed), 3 P3 accepted-not-requested (`[:\s]+` spans newlines — verified `**Complexity Level**:\n\nC2` matches, latent but unreachable via the template; header-before-prose ordering contract documented only in a test docstring; a loose three-way OR'd assertion). Verify was an end-to-end run of the committed hook against the **real** T039/T041 guides + a real copy of the live Kanban, both yielding `C2 | Risk: Medium | P1`.

**Files**: .claude/hooks/post_write_register_task.py, .claude/hooks/tests/test_post_write_register_task_metadata.py (new, 7 tests), tasks/TASK_GUIDE_T042.md

## Infrastructure
